obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.
scoring_text: Knowledge of human-machine interface design for AI oversight, including design of effective oversight mechanisms, human-in-the-loop architectures, and interface tools that enable natural persons to exercise meaningful oversight during AI system operation.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of human-machine interface design for AI oversight, including design of effective oversight mechanisms, human-in-the-loop architectures, and interface tools that enable natural persons to exercise meaningful oversight during AI system operation.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing human oversight mechanisms for AI-assisted decision-making, including escalation triggers, override procedures, human-in-the-loop protocols, and meaningful human control verification.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 14(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.810, cosine: 0.810). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.654), AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.636).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 8.1/10 (raw 0.810). Shared vocabulary: [ai, human, loop, meaningful, mechanisms, oversight]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [architectures, design, effective, enable, exercise, interface] vs WIDAI terms [assisted, control, decision, designing, escalation, making] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.917 (P=0.908, R=0.925). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.810 ≈ STS 0.810 (gap 0.001). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, human, loop, meaningful, mechanisms] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 27.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, human, loop, meaningful, mechanisms, oversight]. EU AI Act-only: [architectures, design, effective, enable, exercise, interface]. WIDAI-only: [assisted, control, decision, designing, escalation, making].
